title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience directly spans AI-powered discovery, search relevance, personalization, and recommendation systems — the exact technical pillars Airbnb needs for homepage transformation. The Samsung.com role (10M+ monthly users, hybrid AI search, conversational AI, recommendation API, CTR and NPS improvements) maps almost perfectly to the "Guest Discovery and Homepage" mandate. The one meaningful gap is consumer travel/marketplace context and lifecycle/growth marketing collaboration, which is a softer requirement but worth addressing in positioning.
recommendation_reason: Strong domain-archetype match (AI discovery + personalization + consumer scale) with quantified evidence at 10M+ user scale; Airbnb tier brand and SF/NY/LA location fit; gap in travel/marketplace domain is minor and bridgeable.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered discovery, search relevance, and personalization experiences at consumer scale — including a hybrid AI search overhaul serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and an ML recommendation system on a $2B+ GMV platform. Passionate about building intuitive browsing experiences that connect users with the right content at the right moment, using the latest AI and ranking technologies.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraging it to launch an add-ons discovery page — boosting AOV by 15%; earlier recommendation system work on a $2B+ GMV platform drove AOV +25%.
gap_handling: Riley has no direct travel/marketplace or two-sided marketplace experience. Mitigate this by drawing explicit parallels: Samsung.com is a multi-category consumer marketplace with international verticals (similar to Homes/Services/Experiences), and the AEO pipeline + conversational AI work demonstrates ability to surface diverse inventory types to users. Briefly acknowledge in cover letter that the discovery mechanics (relevance, ranking, browsing UX) are domain-agnostic and your track record at scale transfers directly.

title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background is an exceptionally strong domain match — hybrid AI search, search ranking, personalization, recommendation systems, and A/B experimentation at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) and Amazon map nearly 1:1 to Airbnb's R&P team mandate. The AEO pipeline, conversational AI chat, and recommendation API work directly mirrors the "natural language query understanding to multi-turn, context-aware discovery" language in the JD. The one honest gap is years: the role asks for 10+ years of PM experience and Riley is at 9, with roughly 3–4 years squarely on ML/AI products — this is a close but real delta that could make the role a slight stretch on paper, though the depth of experience at Samsung likely compensates.
recommendation_reason: Near-perfect domain and archetype alignment (AI search + personalization + e-commerce discovery + A/B experimentation at scale) — one of the strongest natural fits in Riley's job search pipeline.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years specializing in search relevance, personalization, and ML-powered discovery systems at global scale — including hybrid AI search serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven catalog intelligence for 2M+ sellers at Amazon. Proven track record of owning end-to-end roadmaps for technically complex ranking, recommendation, and conversational AI systems, with deep experimentation fluency and a history of measurable marketplace impact.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: The 10+ year requirement vs. Riley's 9 years is a paper gap, not a real one — lead with depth of ML/AI PM ownership (Samsung + Amazon) rather than total tenure. In cover letter or screen, frame the Samsung role as equivalent to a Staff/Lead PM scope (10M+ users, cross-functional AI teams, global rollout) to sidestep the YoE objection. The San Francisco location is a harder constraint — clarify whether remote or hybrid is acceptable before applying, as the posting lists SF as the location with no explicit remote option.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience in e-commerce discovery, personalization, and A/B-driven experimentation maps tightly to Instacart's Shopping Experience mandate — particularly the preferred qualifications around recommendation systems, search, and conversion optimization. The Samsung.com and Kolonmall roles demonstrate end-to-end ownership of consumer-facing discovery funnels at scale (10M+ MAU, $2B+ GMV), directly mirroring the scope of this role. The one notable gap is the Health/nutrition/wellness domain specialization, which is the core focus of this particular product area; Riley has strong discovery and personalization DNA but no explicit health-tech or dietary-filter experience to cite.
recommendation_reason: Strong consumer e-commerce discovery and personalization pedigree with measurable impact at scale clears the bar; health domain gap is manageable given it's a zero-to-one build where product craft matters more.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building consumer discovery and personalization experiences at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon — shipping search, recommendation, and conversion systems used by 10M+ monthly users across global e-commerce platforms. Proven zero-to-one executor with a track record of translating user insights into elegant, data-driven product experiences that measurably improve engagement, conversion, and retention at scale.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 → $185) through iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50%.
gap_handling: The Health domain specialization is the primary gap — address it in the cover letter by connecting dietary/nutrition filtering to your taxonomy optimization and personalization work (e.g., how catalog metadata structuring and guided shopping flows at Samsung translate directly to health-attribute filtering and dietary discovery). Signal genuine interest in the health space and emphasize your zero-to-one track record (AEO pipeline, conversational AI, virtual try-on) to show comfort with ambiguous, greenfield builds.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong domain match — 9 years of search, ranking, and discovery experience across Samsung.com and Amazon maps directly onto Faire's core Search Algorithms mission, including concrete metrics like precision@3 +30% and 1st-result CTR +45%. The primary friction is seniority: this role is titled Staff PM and explicitly requires 7+ years at a "high-growth tech startup or startup founder" — Faire is a Series D/E marketplace startup, and Riley's largest startup credit (Kolonmall) is credible but smaller in scale, while Samsung and Amazon are enterprise environments. The wholesale B2B marketplace domain (retailer-to-brand discovery) is adjacent but not identical to Riley's B2C search experience, which may require tailored framing.
recommendation_reason: Excellent search algorithm depth and metrics-driven evidence, but the Staff-level seniority bar, startup-culture preference, and hybrid NYC/SF requirement create meaningful gaps to address before applying.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered search and discovery systems at scale — including hybrid search ranking, query understanding, and behavioral signal optimization serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record of owning end-to-end search algorithm strategy, defining relevance metrics, and driving measurable outcomes (precision@3 +30%, CTR +45%) in close partnership with ML engineers and data scientists.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: Faire explicitly favors "high-growth tech startup" backgrounds — lean into Kolonmall's $2B+ GMV scale and startup-like agility, and frame Samsung's international search launch as a zero-to-one initiative rather than enterprise maintenance. If applying, a cover letter that directly addresses why a wholesale B2B marketplace discovery problem excites you will matter a lot here.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, strong A/B testing and funnel optimization chops, and 9 years of experience check the core boxes — the Anthropic role explicitly requires AI domain passion and analytical rigor, both of which Riley demonstrates with hard metrics. However, the role is distinctly a Developer Platform / API Growth PM role requiring funnel ownership across onboarding, billing, console, and self-serve upgrade paths for a technical/developer audience — Riley's experience is primarily consumer-facing e-commerce search and discovery, not developer tooling or B2B API platforms. The Amazon stint (B2B seller tools) is the closest proxy but was only ~10 months and focused on listing workflows, not API growth funnels. The growth archetype fit is moderate but developer-centric growth is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain and growth fundamentals, but limited direct developer platform / API funnel experience creates a meaningful gap that will be scrutinized in a competitive Anthropic pipeline.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth across search, discovery, and AI-powered product experiences — including full-funnel optimization, A/B testing, and ML-driven personalization at scale for 10M+ monthly users. Passionate about AI's potential to transform how developers and businesses build, with hands-on experience defining success metrics, shipping LLM-driven pipelines, and partnering with data science and engineering teams to accelerate growth.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification pipelines across 20+ categories (Amazon)

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: The primary gap is developer-facing growth (API onboarding, console UX, billing/upgrade flows, docs). In your cover letter and interviews, lean hard into your Amazon B2B platform experience as the closest analog, and explicitly connect your e-commerce funnel optimization (activation → conversion → retention) to the API growth funnel (sign-up → first API call → paid upgrade). Bonus: your AEO pipeline work demonstrates you understand how developers and AI agents consume structured data programmatically — that's a credible bridge to the developer platform world.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI search and conversational AI background — especially the on-site Copilot/chat experience at Samsung and the AEO/LLM pipeline work — maps well to Twilio's Copilot backend mission. The RAG, contextual AI, and agent orchestration requirements align with skills Riley has demonstrably exercised, and the conversational AI and recommendation API bullets provide credible evidence. However, this role is titled "Principal PM," one level above Riley's target Senior/PM III seniority, and it skews heavily toward backend platform/infrastructure and developer-facing tooling (B2B dev personas, SID error logs, API latency management) rather than Riley's core e-commerce/consumer AI strengths — creating a meaningful archetype gap that will show up in recruiter screens.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM overlap and solid evidence, but the Principal-level seniority and backend platform/developer-tool archetype are both stretches from Riley's consumer e-commerce core.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving intelligent, LLM-powered product experiences — from conversational AI and hybrid search to contextual recommendation engines — at Samsung, Amazon, and high-growth e-commerce platforms. Proven track record designing AI backend systems (knowledge pipelines, ranking models, recommendation APIs) that reduce friction, improve engagement at scale, and deliver measurable business outcomes.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — supporting product discovery and comparison for 10M+ monthly users on Samsung.com

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and conversion by 40% in 12 weeks
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system using multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is backend platform/infrastructure depth for developer personas and the Principal-level seniority bar. Riley should reframe the Samsung conversational AI and AEO pipeline work as backend service ownership (knowledge ingestion, pipeline architecture, latency/performance tradeoffs), and explicitly call out any experience with RAG tooling, API log analysis, or developer-facing products. If RAG was used at Samsung (even evaluating vendors), name it directly. Consider addressing the seniority stretch proactively in a cover letter by citing scope (10M+ users, 3 international markets, cross-org alignment) rather than title.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, LLM pipelines, personalization systems, and A/B experimentation maps closely to the role's technical and domain requirements — especially the RAG/retrieval, contextual personalization, and experiment design expectations. However, this is a Staff PM role focused on a customer support / community support platform, which is a meaningful domain shift away from Riley's e-commerce discovery and search retail expertise; the JD explicitly asks for platform-building experience (foundational systems powering multiple downstream surfaces), where Riley's work skews more toward consumer-facing product. The seniority is also a stretch: Staff PM is one level above Riley's target Senior/PM III tier, and the 9+ years requirement is right at Riley's boundary.
recommendation_reason: Strong technical and AI/personalization alignment, but the Staff-level seniority, customer-support domain pivot, and platform-infrastructure emphasis are meaningful gaps that require deliberate resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping personalization systems, LLM-powered pipelines, and retrieval-based search experiences at consumer scale — including hybrid AI search serving 10M+ monthly users and recommendation APIs that lifted AOV by 25%. Experienced in cross-functional leadership across ML, data science, and engineering to define experiment frameworks, own metrics strategy, and build foundational AI platforms that power multiple downstream surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data — boosting AOV by 15% — and partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals, improving precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The role is squarely in customer support AI (not e-commerce discovery), and emphasizes foundational platform products that serve multiple downstream surfaces — a pattern Riley has touched but not led as a primary charter. In the resume and cover letter, reframe the AEO pipeline, recommendation API, and hybrid search work as platform-building (multi-surface, multi-team infrastructure) rather than product features, and explicitly mention any retrieval system architecture familiarity (RAG, vector search, context window management).

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths — AI-powered search, discovery, personalization, and conversion optimization — partially overlap with this role's "discovery-led Help Center" mandate, and the AI chat/conversational AI work at Samsung is a genuine signal. However, this role is fundamentally about customer support infrastructure, help content platforms, deflection rate optimization, and internal agent tooling — domains Riley has never owned. The Growth context and stakeholder management experience translate well, but the lack of support-platform or help-center PM experience is a real gap that will be visible to Squarespace recruiters.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/discovery/growth transferables, but the core domain (help center redesign, support enablement, agent tooling) is outside Riley's direct experience — apply only with heavy tailoring toward the conversational AI and discovery angles.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years delivering search, discovery, and conversational AI experiences at Samsung.com and Amazon — including a production conversational AI chat experience and hybrid AI search serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record turning fragmented product surfaces into cohesive, metrics-driven discovery platforms that measurably lift conversion, retention, and engagement.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — supporting product discovery and comparison for users across Samsung.com
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing that increased web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows
gap_handling: Riley has no direct help-center or support-platform PM experience — the role's agent tooling and KB management responsibilities are the clearest gaps. In the cover letter and interviews, lean hard into the conversational AI chat experience at Samsung (deflection parallel), the AEO pipeline (structured content at scale), and the discovery-funnel redesign work as analogues for "transforming a siloed platform into a cohesive discovery-led product." Acknowledge the support domain is new territory but frame the AI + discovery + growth combination as rare and directly applicable to the Help Center's evolution.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, strong experimentation discipline, and hands-on experience shipping LLM and conversational AI features align well with Lyft's AI Platform mandate. However, this is a Staff PM role focused on internal AI platform and agentic systems — a Platform/Infrastructure archetype rather than Riley's core Consumer/Search/E-commerce wheelhouse — and it explicitly requires deep multi-agent orchestration and agentic AI experience that Riley's resume only partially evidences (AEO pipeline and conversational AI are adjacent but not the same). The 7+ year requirement is met, but the "Staff" level is a stretch above Riley's target Senior PM band, making the bar higher and more competitive.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML credentials and technical depth are a genuine asset, but the Staff-level Platform/Agentic AI focus is a stretch from Riley's consumer search and discovery core; tailoring is essential.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving technically complex, AI-powered products — from hybrid search and LLM-driven pipelines to conversational AI and recommendation systems at Samsung and Amazon scale. Proven track record leading cross-functional teams of engineers and scientists, defining platform metrics and KPIs, and evangelizing AI adoption across organizations serving 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — directly adjacent to agentic AI platform work
Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system with multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is explicit agentic AI / multi-agent orchestration experience — the role's centerpiece. Riley should reframe the AEO pipeline (automated metadata extraction, structured output agents) and conversational AI work as multi-agent coordination in the cover letter and resume. If Riley has any side projects, coursework, or prototyping experience with LangChain, AutoGen, or similar agentic frameworks, that should be added to the Skills section under AI/ML.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. However, the role explicitly calls for a "former consumer founder" who spikes on 0-to-1 product creation — Riley's background is primarily 1-to-N optimization and feature scaling at established companies (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon), not greenfield consumer product invention or startup founding. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — including conversational AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of translating emerging model capabilities into products people love. Deeply hands-on across the full product lifecycle from vision and prototyping through scaled global launch, with a bias for creative, low-cost experimentation to validate product-market fit fast.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

Launched hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley has no stated startup founding experience — the single biggest filter for this role. To address this, the cover letter should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat launches as 0-to-1 entrepreneurial bets driven by Riley's initiative, not top-down roadmap items. If Riley has any side projects, independent AI product experiments, or early-stage advisory work, those should be surfaced prominently. Leaning into hands-on prototyping with Claude or other AI tools (per the JD's "Claude Code" signal) would also help close the founder-proxy gap.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials and solid A/B testing and analytics experience that partially maps to this role, but the core ask is Growth/Monetization PM — specifically pricing & packaging, free-to-paid conversion, and subscription funnel optimization — which is a distinct archetype from Riley's AI Search and Discovery focus. The Amazon and Kolon roles show some growth-adjacent work (onboarding flows, retention, AOV uplift), but none is explicitly monetization or subscription conversion ownership. The role explicitly demands "majority in growth focused roles," which Riley's 9-year arc does not clearly demonstrate.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain fit and brand upside at Anthropic, but the monetization/growth PM archetype is a meaningful stretch from Riley's core search & discovery identity — needs deliberate resume reframing to compete.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth through data-driven experimentation, personalization, and conversion funnel optimization on platforms serving 10M+ users and $2B+ GMV. Proven track record of owning end-to-end product strategy across AI-powered experiences — including onboarding, retention, and AOV growth — and a passion for building safe, beneficial AI products that scale.
top_bullets: Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program and boosted 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for 2M+ B2B sellers, reducing manual contacts with support agents through targeted UX improvements informed by 10+ interviews and 300+ surveys
gap_handling: Riley lacks explicit monetization ownership (pricing tiers, paywall design, free-to-paid conversion). In the cover letter and resume, reframe the Kolon onboarding/retention work as a "growth and monetization" initiative with hard conversion metrics. If possible, quantify subscription or paid-tier impact at Samsung.com (e.g., any paid plan or premium feature gating). Be ready to speak credibly in interviews about pricing strategy frameworks — Anthropic will probe this directly.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops, SQL fluency, and hands-on experience with LLM pipelines (AEO, conversational AI, recommendation APIs) are genuine strengths that map well to Datadog's AI-powered query layer. However, the role is fundamentally rooted in the modern data stack — BI tools, semantic layers, data warehouses, governance consoles — a domain where Riley has limited direct exposure; the experience skews e-commerce/search discovery rather than analytics infrastructure or B2B SaaS data products. The 0-to-1, enterprise-facing, observability-adjacent scope is also a meaningful pivot from Riley's consumer/retail background, and the NYC in-office hybrid requirement introduces a location mismatch from Bellevue, WA.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credentials and SQL skills are genuine fits, but lack of BI/data stack domain experience and NYC location requirement are real hurdles that require deliberate framing.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and data-driven discovery systems at scale — including a structured metadata extraction pipeline that drove 8x AI agent traffic at Samsung.com. Experienced shipping AI products with rigorous evaluation frameworks, experimentation, and cross-functional delivery in complex B2B and consumer environments, with strong SQL fluency and a track record of aligning technical and executive stakeholders around zero-to-one bets.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ high-impact categories
gap_handling: Riley has no direct BI/semantic layer or data warehouse PM experience — the JD explicitly calls out knowledge of tools like dbt, Looker, Snowflake, etc. as core domain knowledge. To bridge this gap: (1) explicitly reference the AEO pipeline as a "structured data extraction and semantic enrichment layer" in resume language; (2) draw a direct analogy between the PIM-to-schema-markup pipeline and a semantic/context layer for AI queries; (3) in the cover letter, acknowledge the observability-data angle as a new domain while positioning the AI governance and eval framework experience (search relevance metrics, precision@3, instrumentation strategy) as transferable rigor for building trustworthy AI analytics.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Product Manager, Claude Code
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials with proven impact at scale — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, AEO, and conversational AI at Samsung.com all demonstrate genuine AI product depth. However, the Claude Code role has a hard requirement of 1+ year as a professional software engineer, which is not evidenced anywhere in Riley's resume; the background is business analytics and PM, not SWE. The role is squarely in developer tooling (CLI, agentic coding assistants), a niche Riley has no documented experience in — a meaningful domain gap versus e-commerce discovery and search.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product pedigree and sponsorship-friendly company, but the required engineering background and dev-tools domain are real gaps that make this a competitive long shot.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-driven pipelines, conversational AI, and agentic search systems at Samsung.com and Amazon — products used by tens of millions of users and thousands of sellers. Passionate about translating frontier model capabilities into practical, high-leverage developer experiences and equally comfortable debating relevance metrics with ML researchers or reviewing experiment results with engineering.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The JD explicitly asks for at least 1 year of professional engineering experience. Riley's resume shows no SWE tenure — only PM and analytics roles. If you apply, address this directly in a cover letter: highlight any hands-on technical work (SQL, BigQuery, prompt engineering, pipeline design) and frame your close partnership with ML/science teams as a proxy for technical depth. Consider noting any coding projects or tools built outside of formal PM roles. Without this, screeners may filter the application early.

title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product credentials are strong — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, search ranking, and recommendation systems all map well to the "AI-native product" language in the JD. Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows, and multi-agent signals — that are directionally relevant. However, this role is squarely in enterprise identity security (IAM, OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust), a domain Riley has no documented experience in, which is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. Additionally, the role is developer-platform / B2B enterprise, a departure from Riley's consumer/e-commerce core. The LLM and AI agent awareness partially bridges the gap, but the identity infrastructure expertise is a meaningful missing piece.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product background is genuinely relevant, but the IAM/OAuth/OIDC domain expertise is a hard requirement Riley can't currently satisfy — needs honest gap acknowledgment.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and multi-agent workflows at Samsung and Amazon, reaching 10M+ monthly users. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting AI-driven product discovery for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Before applying, Riley should invest a few hours in self-study — read Okta's developer docs on OAuth2/OIDC flows, skim the emerging MCP (Model Context Protocol) spec, and reference any API design or token-based auth work (even indirectly) in the resume. In the cover letter, frame the AEO pipeline and multi-agent workflow familiarity as a direct bridge to AI agent governance, and be upfront that the identity security domain is a learning edge — Okta may value the fresh AI product instincts over a traditional IAM background for this zero-to-one charter.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley's core PM strengths — cross-functional leadership, data-driven roadmapping, A/B testing, and AI/ML product delivery — are genuinely transferable, and the seniority level is a solid match.
